methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $33M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Space & Aerospace (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 23 same-sector peers exist in Space & Aerospace, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Space & Aerospace P/S median is 45.3x across 6 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

data.narrative:
Radian Aerospace is a Seed-stage space and aerospace company that has raised $33M to date. The valuation framework draws on a comp universe of 6 public Space & Aerospace peers (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL) and 8 private peers including SpaceX, Rocket Lab, and Blue Origin, though few operate at identical stage and scale.

Of the three triangulation methods considered, only Stage-Progression applies with sufficient rigor. This method uses the $33M total raised as a proxy for current valuation and applies a 2.5x multiplier typical of Seed-to-next-stage progression in venture markets. This yields an implied valuation of $82M. The Peer-Median approach could not be applied: while 23 same-sector peers exist, narrowing to same-stage, adjacent-stage, and similar-tier companies produced fewer than 3 datapoints. Public-Comp Anchoring is not feasible because Radian's revenue is not disclosed; the sector median P/S of 45.3x across public comparables cannot be applied without that figure.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $70M to $95M, centered on $82M. This estimate carries material uncertainty. The single-method foundation reflects Stage-Progression's reliance on a raised-capital proxy rather than operational metrics. Revenue, unit economics, and technical validation status—typical drivers of frontier-tech company value—are not provided and would materially refine this assessment.
